records.ety: [Acc. of L. requies rest, the first words of the Mass being “Requiem aeternam dona eis, Domine,” give eternal rest to them, O lord; pref. re- re + quies quiet. See Quiet, n., and cf. Requin.]

defs: A mass said or sung for the repose of a departed soul.
quotes: We should profane the service of the dead To sing a requiem and such rest to her As to peace-parted souls.

defs: Any grand musical composition, performed in honor of a deceased person.

quotes: Else had I an eternal requiem kept, And in the arms of peace forever slept.
